executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 101 Canadian organizations victimized in a single 48-hour period. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit by both Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked by Everest), and Eastern Townships School Board. The Qilin ransomware group alone is responsible for 18 victims, followed by Clop with 13, Play with 10, and SafePay with 10. Critical infrastructure sectors hit hardest include healthcare, education, financial services, and municipal government (Municipality of North Perth).

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a critical ransomware crisis with 101 organizations compromised across all sectors in 48 hours. Healthcare facilities (Markham Stouffville Hospital, BioPharma Services, Labtician Ophthalmics, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, Thunder Bay Counselling), educational institutions (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, teccart.qc.ca), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and critical infrastructure have been severely impacted. No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by both Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters gangs (dual breach), Active Green + Ross hit by Sinobi, Kids & Company (childcare services) breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company database leaked by Everest ransomware gang, Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi, Pewarchuk CPA (accounting firm) hit by Play, MG Chartered Professional Accountant breached by Qilin

report_2w.executive_summary: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 is now under active exploitation and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog—this is the most urgent threat requiring immediate patching. An Anthropic AI researcher discovered this critical RCE flaw, and maintainers patched it in seven days, but attackers are already leveraging it. Additionally, Canada's CCCS has issued Alert AL26-007 for a vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616). Cisco has released patches for four critical flaws in Identity Services Engine and Webex enabling remote code execution. The Amtrak breach exposed 2.1 million accounts, allegedly by ShinyHunters, and McGraw Hill suffered a 100GB+ data breach following an extortion attempt.

items.description: Remote code execution vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ discovered by Anthropic AI researcher
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability in end-of-life TP-Link Wi-Fi routers under active exploitation attempts

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 on April 7, 2026 regarding CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS, requiring immediate attention from Canadian organizations using this product. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period, though Canadian organizations remain exposed to the actively exploited Apache ActiveMQ and Cisco vulnerabilities affecting enterprises globally.
